rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by adding contract content only within a valid existing owner scope.
Keep component-type contract content separate from implementation-level content; do not move architecture elements across scopes.
Use only identifiers that are grounded in the current model or explicitly declared within the inserted content.
generic_rules: Maintain original features, subcomponents, connections, and properties unchanged when inserting annex content.
Respect owner boundaries: type-level port-facing contract content belongs with the component type, while implementation-specific bindings belong only in implementation scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is enclosed in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Implementation-level additions must be valid after relevant subcomponents and connections are already in scope.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports from a component type scope without a valid exposed variable or output.
Do not treat AADL property names or unit tokens as in-scope AGREE variables.
Do not invent or rename model identifiers during fusion.
irrelevant_topics: Requirement-to-clause selection for the current case
Case-specific contract semantics or repair decisions
